title: Viking Therapeutics Inc (VKTX) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Strategic Advances Amid Rising Expenses
text: Viking Therapeutics reported significant progress across its drug pipeline in 2024, marked by strong clinical results and improved financial positioning. The company's net loss widened to $110 million for 2024, compared to $85.9 million the prior year. Research and development expenses nearly doubled to $101.6 million from $63.8 million, while general and administrative costs climbed to $49.3 million from $37 million. However, Viking strengthened its balance sheet substantially, closing 2024 with over $900 million in cash after raising more than $630 million through a public stock offering. Viking achieved positive data from the VK2735 subcutaneous and oral tablet programs for obesity. The VK2809 program for metabolic dysfunction-associated fatty liver disease demonstrated success across primary and secondary endpoints, showing meaningful improvements in liver fat reduction and fibrosis resolution. Additionally, the company's VK0214 candidate for X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy met safety and tolerability objectives in Phase 1b testing. The company plans to initiate Phase 3 trials for VK2735 in obesity in the second quarter of 2025. Leadership indicated Phase 3 studies will enroll approximately 4,500 participants across two populations: individuals with obesity and those with obesity plus type 2 diabetes.
